title: C1: Godot walking skeleton + scenario-runner see-harness
description: GDScript GameState model; render 10x8 board + 20 pieces/player; scenario runner (--scenario <json>) that loads a state, renders one frame, saves .qa/frame.png + .qa/state.json, quits. Proves the Godot see-loop on real state. Probe (main.gd) is the screenshot reference.
close_reason: Godot walking skeleton + scenario-runner see-harness; see-loop proven on real state

title: B2a: finalize core contract (types/height/board/orbs) — green web-ci
description: Lead drafted web/src/core/{types,height,board,orbs}.ts (uncommitted). Finalize: fix board.test.ts GameState fixtures to new shape, add height.test.ts + orbs.test.ts, web-ci green, commit. Do NOT change core logic (it is the contract).
close_reason: Core contract finalized, 62 tests

title: Track B1: web walking skeleton + prove the /qa see-loop
description: Phaser3+TS+Vite. Render 10x8 board + pieces at localhost; window.NNQR={state,api}; select->valid moves->move+capture. Validate loop: navigate->screenshot->evaluate state->click->assert. First Gherkin story.
close_reason: Web walking skeleton + see-loop proven (PR pending)

title: Godot isometric renderer + window stretch/fill
description: Two issues found by playing the build: (1) board renders 860x560 top-left in a large window with dead grey space — project.godot has no stretch mode (add window/stretch/mode=canvas_items + aspect=keep so the design scales+centers). (2) renderer is flat top-down (ported from web); make it isometric (2:1 diamonds) using the 0-4 height system as real elevation (2.5D). Must keep pixel_to_tile inverse-projection correct for clicking, all overlays (selection/moves/orbs/power-target/win/HUD), and reposition the power menu for the iso board bounds. Validate via see-harness PNGs + live window.
close_reason: Isometric renderer (2:1 diamonds, height-as-elevation 2.5D) + window stretch-to-fill. ba8be7f. 13/13 + 5/5 green; 3 iso renders visually validated by lead (orbs shows elevation, collect_menu shows board+menu, hud shows counts). Window-fill + click mapping are verify-live.

title: C5 (godot): interactive parity gaps vs web — title/mode-select, pause/leave, HUD polish
description:
Parity audit of web controller/main vs godot found these MISSING/PARTIAL (core gameplay is all WIRED + 5 user stories pass). NONE were in original C4 scope. Prioritized:
1. Title scene + game-mode selection: hotseat (2-player) + AI difficulty (easy/hard/expert) are unreachable — main.gd hard-codes VS_AI=true, DIFFICULTY=medium. ai_take_turn already supports all 4 difficulties; just needs UI.
2. Pause/leave overlay (Esc to pause, Resume/Quit-to-title) — OPERATOR EXPLICITLY WANTED pause/leave; absent in godot. (Esc-cancel-targeting + random seed + win-restart-hint already fixed in 6c80bc0.)
3. HUD polish: piece counts (P1:N P2:N), AI-thinking indicator, overheat warning (warn/red at >=7) in power menu rows, number-key 1-9 power shortcuts, targeting-mode instructions text.
Reference: web/src/main.ts (title/pause), web/src/game/{controller,renderer,pause-overlay,title}.ts. Lead has the full audit table.
close_reason: C5 done (55468f7): title screen + mode (vs-AI/hotseat) + difficulty select; pause/leave overlay (Esc, Resume, Quit-to-Title); HUD piece counts + AI-thinking + mode label; power menu number labels + overheat ⚠ + targeting instructions; number-key 1-9 shortcuts; random new-game seed. 13/13 unit + 5/5 QA stories green; 4 views visually validated. Built by 2 parallel workers (shell + HUD) on the load_state hud_info contract.

title: Add Godot CI job to GitHub Actions
description: Run godot/tests/run_all.sh headless on Linux. No godot job in CI today; needed as remote merge gate for feature/godot.
close_reason: Godot CI job added (5b8df1b) — runs 13 headless unit tests on ubuntu-latest with cached binary. VERIFIED green on the real GH runner (23s); all 3 jobs pass on PR #13. Scenario rendering omitted from CI (needs GPU; stays a local see-loop tool).

title: Phase 0: power dispatch auto-generation + correctness tests
description: Auto-generate PowerExecutor.DISPATCH from Powers.definitions (drift-proof); dispatch_completeness_spec + powers_overheat_spec; doc 83->82. Delivered in PR #9.
close_reason: Done in PR #9 (commit 60294a6)

title: Phase 1: data-driven power effects (decompose power_effects.lua)
description:
## Objective
Collapse the 57 row/col/radial power variants (~75% duplicate bodies) in src/shared/power_effects.lua into a data-driven area-selector pattern; split the 2398-line file by category.

## Context
Phase 0 made PowerExecutor.DISPATCH auto-generated (PR #9), so data-driven powers slot into the naming convention. Existing spec/power_effects_spec.lua + spec/power_executor/* are the behavior-preserving safety net.

## Acceptance criteria
- applyAreaEffect(state, piece, selector, effect) with selectors rowTiles/columnTiles/radial3x3
- 57 variant powers expressed as {area, effect} data
- power_effects.lua split into src/shared/powers/effects/{category}.lua, thin aggregator preserves public surface
- All existing power specs stay green; mise run -C lua/love2d ci green
close_reason: Phase 1 merged (PR #10)

title: Phase 2: decompose game.lua (3349-line god object)
description:
## Objective
Extract input_handler, screen_renderer, game_controller from src/game.lua; encapsulate the 14+ module-level mutables into Game.state = {logic, ui, visual, input}.

## Context
game.lua has no spec (Love2D-coupled). Extract pure logic to testable modules; the Love2D layer is verified by running the game.

## Acceptance criteria
- src/client/{input_handler,screen_renderer,game_controller}.lua extracted
- Extracted pure logic has specs; game.lua is a thin coordinator
- mise run -C lua/love2d ci green
- MANUAL: mise run love-start -> menu -> local game -> power use -> game over works
close_reason: Phase 2 merged (PR #11) + centerpult fix

title: Phase 3: AI activates powers
description:
## Objective
Wire power activation into the AI action space (ai.lua/search.lua/evaluator.lua) so Hard/Expert opponents use their 82 powers, not just piece moves.

## Context
evaluator.lua already has shouldUseJumpProof/shouldUseDestroyRow/getCaptureOpportunities/getThreatenedPieces hooks but they are never dispatched. Depends on Phase 1's uniform PowerExecutor.execute interface.

## Acceptance criteria
- AI candidate set = moves + power activations
- Heuristic + minimax (capped breadth) consider powers; no wasted single-use powers
- Tests: picks destroy_row when captures >=2; jump_proof only when threatened; expert search within turn-time budget

title: Phase 4: finish multiplayer (server authoritative)
description:
## Objective
Implement the server TODOs and close the untested multiplayer.lua bridge gap.

## Context
server/server.lua TODOs: :311 real GameLogic session, :520 ACTIVATE_POWER, :526 CHAT. Server-side 60s reconnect timeout stored but not enforced. src/client/multiplayer.lua has zero specs.

## Acceptance criteria
- GameLogic-backed session; server validates moves; ACTIVATE_POWER via PowerExecutor broadcasts POWER_RESULT; CHAT broadcasts CHAT_MESSAGE
- 60s reconnect timeout enforced server-side
- spec/multiplayer_spec.lua integration coverage (connect->lobby->sync->move->disconnect->reconnect)
- server/server_state.json gitignored + untracked

title: export-state/ directory should be in .gitignore
description:
The export-state/ directory (added in v0.49.0 for per-worktree export state tracking) contains machine-specific paths (worktree_root) and local sync state. It should be added to the default .beads/.gitignore template since per-worktree state shouldn't be tracked in git.

Current .beads/.gitignore ignores similar local state files (sync-state.json, last-touched, redirect) but not export-state/.

File contents example:
```json
{
  "worktree_root": "/Users/vinnie/github/nnqr",
  "last_export_commit": "a5ee4608dd741d5b370292c8f1f55f00efd972ae",
  "last_export_time": "2026-01-25T09:35:26.33143-05:00",
  "jsonl_hash": "a0d84af5806a678a2ee26658bac31c61058f4fcde931b81d02a5dbf220c58cc9"
}
```
close_reason: Obsolete: bd .beads/export-state removed in migration to bees
